content.problemStatement: Identify and rectify the errors in the code provided to get the desired output.
content.inputFormat: First line may contain an integer T, the number of repetitions. If the line is missing or empty, T defaults to 1.
content.outputFormat: Print the three lines in order: "Coding Chef", "Chafe", "Coding Chafe". Repeat this block T times, each line separated by a newline. The output ends with a newline after the last line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: No input is provided, so the program defaults to T = 1 and prints the three lines once.

sampleTestCases.output:
Coding Chef
Chafe
Coding Chafe
Coding Chef
Chafe
Coding Chafe
sampleTestCases.explanation: T = 2, therefore the block of three lines is printed twice.
T = 1, the block is printed exactly once.

solution.approach: Read the first line from standard input. If it is missing, empty or cannot be parsed as an integer, treat T as 1. Otherwise parse T. Use a StringBuilder to append the fixed three‑line block T times and finally print the accumulated string. This runs in O(T) time and O(1) auxiliary space (apart from the output buffer).
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) time, where T is the number of repetitions.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space (output buffer not counted).
